title: OSP Profile: Energy Supply v1
description: Vocabulary for retail and wholesale energy supply offers: electricity, gas, heat, cooling, and hydrogen. Designed so an agent can compare providers on the parameters that actually drive procurement decisions — supply mix, carbon intensity, tariff model, and consumption fit — not only on headline price.

commodity.description: The energy commodity being supplied. One commodity per manifest — multi-commodity providers use multiple manifests.

consumer_segments.description: Customer classes the provider serves. Agents should match this against the requester's own classification before any price comparison.

supply_mix.description: Declared breakdown of generation or procurement sources. Shares are expressed as fractions of 1 and should sum to ~1.0.

renewable_share.description: Fraction of the supply that is renewable. Convenience aggregate of supply_mix; providers should publish both.

carbon_intensity_gco2_per_kwh.description: Grams of CO2-equivalent per delivered kWh, computed over the declared supply mix.

green_certifications.description: Third-party verified green-energy labels. Required for credible renewable claims in most jurisdictions.

tariff_model.description: Pricing structure. Spot and dynamic pass market volatility to the customer; fixed shifts it to the supplier.

price_index.description: Reference index for indexed or spot tariffs. Example: 'EEX Day-Ahead DE-LU', 'TTF month-ahead'.

annual_consumption_range_kwh.description: Accepted annual volume in kWh. Hard filter for whether the offer applies.

voltage_levels_served.description: Electrical supply only. Determines whether the provider can serve the grid connection in question.

balancing_group_managed.description: Whether the provider manages the customer's balancing group responsibility. Relevant for mid-to-large industrial supply.

smart_meter_required.description: Whether the offer requires a smart meter to be installed at the consumption site.

dynamic_pricing_supported.description: Whether hourly (or finer) dynamic pricing is supported end-to-end, including metering and billing.

supply_start_leadtime.description: Typical time between contract signature and supply start. Example: '30 days', 'next billing cycle'.

termination_notice.description: Required notice period to terminate an active contract. Example: '3 months'.

local_grid_operator_independent.description: Whether the supply offer is decoupled from the local DSO (i.e., customer can switch without grid changes).
